evals.prompt: I need to add a `max_background_agents` config option to oh-my-opencode that limits how many background agents can run simultaneously. It should be in the plugin config schema with a default of 5. Add validation and make sure the background manager respects it. Create a PR for this.
evals.expected_output: Agent creates worktree, implements config option with schema validation, adds tests, creates PR, iterates through verification gates until merged

assertions.text: Plan uses git worktree in a sibling directory (not main working directory)
Branch is created from origin/dev (not master/main)
Plan specifies multiple atomic commits for multi-file changes
Runs bun run typecheck, bun test, and bun run build before pushing
PR is created targeting dev branch (not master)
Verification loop includes all 3 gates: CI, review-work, and Cubic
Gates are checked in order: CI first, then review-work, then Cubic
Cubic check uses gh api to check cubic-dev-ai[bot] reviews for 'No issues found'
Plan includes worktree cleanup after merge
Code changes reference actual files in the codebase (config schema, background manager)

evals.prompt: The atlas hook has a bug where it crashes when boulder.json is missing the worktree_path field. Fix it and land the fix as a PR. Make sure CI passes.
evals.expected_output: Agent creates worktree for the fix branch, adds null check and test for missing worktree_path, creates PR, iterates verification loop

assertions.text: Plan uses git worktree in a sibling directory
Fix is minimal — adds null check, doesn't refactor unrelated code
Test case added for the missing worktree_path scenario
Verification loop includes all 3 gates: CI, review-work, Cubic
References actual atlas hook files in src/hooks/atlas/
Branch name follows fix/ prefix convention

evals.prompt: Refactor src/tools/delegate-task/constants.ts to split DEFAULT_CATEGORIES and CATEGORY_MODEL_REQUIREMENTS into separate files. Keep backward compatibility with the barrel export. Make a PR.
evals.expected_output: Agent creates worktree, splits file with atomic commits, ensures imports still work via barrel, creates PR, runs through all gates

assertions.text: Plan uses git worktree in a sibling directory
Uses 2+ commits for the multi-file refactor
Maintains backward compatibility via barrel re-export in constants.ts or index.ts
Verification loop includes all 3 gates
References actual src/tools/delegate-task/constants.ts file and its exports

evals.prompt: implement issue #100 - we need to add a new built-in MCP for arxiv paper search. just the basic search endpoint, nothing fancy. pr it
evals.expected_output: Agent creates worktree, implements arxiv MCP following existing MCP patterns (websearch, context7, grep_app), creates PR with proper template, verification loop runs

assertions.text: Plan uses git worktree in a sibling directory
New MCP follows existing pattern from src/mcp/ (websearch, context7, grep_app)
Verification loop includes all 3 gates
PR targets dev branch
Runs local checks before pushing

evals.prompt: The comment-checker hook is too aggressive - it's flagging legitimate comments that happen to contain 'Note:' as AI slop. Relax the regex pattern and add test cases for the false positives. Work on a separate branch and make a PR.
evals.expected_output: Agent creates worktree, fixes regex, adds specific test cases for false positive scenarios, creates PR, all three gates pass

assertions.text: Plan uses git worktree in a sibling directory
References actual comment-checker hook files in the codebase
Adds test cases specifically for 'Note:' false positive scenarios
Verification loop includes all 3 gates
Only modifies regex and adds tests — no unrelated changes
